channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Donald Trump and Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp use hurricane recovery to make first appearance together
item.description: After making up privately, former President Donald Trump and Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp are using the aftermath of Hurricane Helene to put their detente on public display with a month to go before Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump national security advisors mock Biden's warnings to Israel to stick to ‘proportional’ Iran response
item.description: The Biden administration has now shifted its priority to containment, helping the region avoid all-out war between its two hegemonic superpowers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden says he doesn't know whether Israel is holding up peace deal to influence 2024 US election
item.description: President Joe Biden has offered some terse words for Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, saying he doesn't know whether the Israeli leader is holding up a peace deal in order to influence the outcome of the 2024 U.S. presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Long, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Tim Walz to make fundraising trip to California
item.description: (FOX40.COM) — Minnesota governor and Democratic Vice Presidential Candidate Tim Walz is expected to visit several California cities starting this weekend as part of campaign fundraising efforts. On Sunday afternoon Walz will speak in San Diego and Montecito before heading to Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Jacque Porter, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Blackface photo shakes up toss-up House district in NY
item.description: New York Republican Congressman Michael Lawler is embroiled in controversy over a 2006 Halloween costume, when he dressed in blackface as Michael Jackson.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Elon Musk: LA residents recoil at mention of Trump's name
item.description: Elon Musk quips that people in Los Angeles have a strong negative response to the topic of former President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump set to hold 'emotional' rally in Butler, site of assassination attempt
item.description: Former President Trump is set to return to Butler, Pa., for what is likely to be a highly charged rally, nearly three months after a bullet grazed his ear at the same site in an attempted assassination.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House Republican apologizes for wearing blackface at 2006 Halloween party
item.description: Rep. Mike Lawler (R-N.Y.) apologized late Thursday night for wearing blackface as part of a Halloween costume portraying pop star Michael Jackson in 2006 during a college party.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court to weigh a Texas death row case after halting execution
item.description: The U.S. Supreme Court will hear the case of a Texas man on death row who has long argued that DNA testing would help prove he didn't kill an 85-year-old woman during a home robbery decades ago.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Whitehurst, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: New Yorkers think Eric Adams should resign after corruption indictment: poll
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Nearly 70% of New York City residents think Mayor Eric Adams should resign following his corruption indictment, according to a Marist College poll released Friday. Another 63% of New Yorkers think Gov. Kathy Hochul should remove Adams from his position if he does not resign.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Biden student loan cancellation plan on hold again
item.description: The Biden administration’s student loan cancellation plan has been put on hold once again in Missouri after brief celebration from advocates this week when another judge ruled the plan could move forward.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Assassination attempts and new threats have reshaped how Donald Trump campaigns
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Donald Trump was onstage at a rally on Long Island last month, talking about taxes, when he appeared momentarily spooked by something he'd spotted over his shoulder. “I thought this was a wise guy coming up,” he explained, joking that he was getting his elbow ready to fight back.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump to headline NRA event in pivotal swing state two weeks before Election Day
item.description: Former President Trump will headline the NRA's Defend the 2nd event in Savannah, Georgia, Oct. 22, rallying support for the Second Amendment two weeks before Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Assemblyman Eddie Gibbs arrested during traffic stop: NYPD
item.description: EAST HARLEM, Manhattan (PIX11) – New York state Assemblyman Eddie Gibbs was arrested during a traffic stop in Manhattan on Thursday, according to the NYPD. The East Harlem Democrat was taken into custody in front of 1844 Lexington Ave. around 1:30 p.m., police said.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Disapproval mounts both at home and abroad as US avoids direct action against Houthi rebels
item.description: While much of the world has eyes on Israel’s battles with Hezbollah and Hamas, the U.S. Navy has its sights set on another of Iran’s proxies, the Yemeni Houthi rebels.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Californians' crime concerns put pressure on criminal justice reform and progressive DAs
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Ten years ago, Alley Bean joined 3.7 million Californians in voting for a measure that downgraded many nonviolent felony crimes to misdemeanors, such as petty shoplifting and drug use, hoping it would lead to a more equitable criminal justice system and help end mass incarceration
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING and TRÂN NGUYỄN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Springsteen calls Trump 'most dangerous candidate for president in my lifetime,' backs Harris
item.description: The rock legend said the former president “doesn’t understand the meaning of this country, its history or what it means to be deeply American.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pressure building on Mayor Eric Adams following federal indictment
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Pressure continues to build on Mayor Eric Adams -- with more federal corruption charges possible. On Thursday, the mayor seems to have forced out his schools chancellor sooner than expected, with the governor telling him to make sure he keeps the city running smoothly.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Melania Trump says she supports abortion rights, putting her at odds with the GOP
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Melania Trump revealed her support for abortion rights Thursday ahead of the release of her upcoming memoir, exposing a stark contrast with her husband, Republican presidential nominee Donald Trump, on the crucial election issue.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Penn. Republican Senate candidate David McCormick: 'I'm the underdog'
item.description: “I'm the underdog,” David McCormick told NewsNation’s Leland Vittert in a one-on-one interview for “On Balance.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Photo shows U.S. Rep. Mike Lawler wearing blackface at college Halloween party in 2006
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (AP) — U.S. Rep. Mike Lawler said he was sorry Thursday after the New York Times obtained photos of him wearing blackface about two decades ago at a college Halloween costume party where he dressed as Michael Jackson.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minneapolis mayor, City Council clash on homeless shelter funding
item.description: The Minneapolis City Council is launching an inquiry into the city administration’s financial reporting practices following a dispute over funding for a homeless shelter.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Debate performance gives Vance 2028 White House boost
item.description: Sen. JD Vance’s (R-Ohio) debate performance is giving him a shot in the arm to become a leading 2028 presidential contender no matter what happens in November between former President Trump and Vice President Harris.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Harris-Walz campaign kicks off a nationwide effort to reach Native voters
item.description: Tribal leaders from across the country showed their support for the democratic ticket at Mystic Lake Event Center in Prior Lake on Wednesday. Native American civic leaders say the Harris-Walz campaign is prepared to support issues that impact Indigenous communities.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Olson, MPR News

item.title: Are Nevada voters swayed by promises of 'no tax on tips'?
item.description: Reporter John Langeler from KLAS in Las Vegas joins "The Hill" to discuss whether Nevada voters are swayed by proposals to eliminate federal taxes on tips.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Prosecutors lay out new evidence in Trump election case, accuse him of having 'resorted to crimes'
item.description: A court filing unsealed Wednesday argues that the former president is not entitled to immunity from prosecution over his failed bid to remain in power.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump rejects calls for another debate with Harris after VP showdown
item.description: Former President Donald Trump again rejected the prospect of another debate with Vice President Kamala Harris late Tuesday following a showdown between the two vice presidential nominees, saying he was "not looking to do it again.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Memorable moments from the vice presidential debate
item.description: Vice presidential nominees Sen. JD Vance (R-Ohio) and Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D) clashed Tuesday in what is likely to be the last debate of this presidential election season.
item.dc:creator: Sarakshi Rai, WGN 9

item.title: From Walz’s hometown to an American Legion, Minnesotans react to governor in VP debate
item.description: Supporters of the Republican and Democratic presidential tickets gathered Tuesday evening for debate watch parties around Minnesota for the likely only debate between Sen. J.D. Vance of Ohio and Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, Matt Sepic, and Nicole Ki, MPR News

item.title: Walz, Vance double down on dueling abortion views
item.description: November 2024 will mark the first U.S. presidential election since the Supreme Court overturned its decision in Roe v. Wade.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Are convicted felons allowed to vote in Illinois?
item.description: With just five weeks until the 2024 presidential election, most U.S. citizens over the age of 18 are eligible to vote as long as they are registered. However, for some individuals, the right to vote may be more complicated due to a criminal conviction.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, WGN 9

item.title: Vance and Walz are both veterans. Here's what it means to have a vet VP
item.description: For the first time since 1996, both major parties have picked a veteran for vice president — a fact that some veterans' groups are hoping it leads to greater understanding of military and vet issues.
item.dc:creator: Quil Lawrence, MPR News

item.title: Trump suggests ‘1 rough hour’ of policing would end retail crime
item.description: The former president called for law enforcement to be freed up to "do their job" without restrictions.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Biden administration expands asylum restrictions at southern border
item.description: The Biden administration announced Monday it will expand its asylum restrictions at the U.S.-Mexico border, cementing tougher standards that will cut off access to protections for many migrants.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Harris trolls Trump at Vegas rally and LA fundraiser, says her crowds are 'pretty big'
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris poked at Donald Trump over crowd sizes, his refusal to debate again and his privileged background on Sunday as she hauled in campaign cash in California and held a raucous rally at the same Nevada venue where the Republican nominee had appeared just two
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: VIDEO: Donald Trump tosses chicken tenders into crowd at college football game
item.description: Former President Donald Trump surprised college students on Saturday in Alabama with an unusual gift for football fans.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Here are the rules for the Vance-Walz debate
item.description: The debate, which will kick off at 9 p.m. EDT on Tuesday from the network’s New York office, will be moderated by CBS anchor Norah O’Donnell and "Face the Nation" host Margaret Brennan.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC
